subject: GRB 200506B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 04:59:44 UT on 6 May 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200506B (trigger 610433989.715966 / 200506208).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 82.2, Dec = -6.7 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 05h 28m, -6d 42'), with a statistical uncertainty of 15.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 102.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200506208/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200506208.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200506208/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200506208.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200506208/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200506208.gif
